headline: Iranians live under constant anxiety: 'When I close my eyes, I wonder if I will see the next day'
description: Bombings, repression and daily surveillance have thrust Iranians into a state of uncertainty. The fear that the end of the war might leave the regime intact looms large in people's minds.

headline: Iran's Revolutionary Guard threatens US universities in the Middle East
Anti-Trump protests kick off on 'No Kings' day in US
The American dream crumbles for French students
description: Since Donald Trump's return to the White House, several institutions have reported a decline in applications to US campuses. Amid political tensions, stricter visa scrutiny and a shift favoring colleges in Asia, a subtle downturn is taking shape.

headline: Revealed: Satellite image shows mysterious cargo at Iranian nuclear site in 2025
description: Le Monde reveals an image of a convoy at the gates of a major underground nuclear complex in Iran. According to several experts, it could be part of the country's stockpile of enriched uranium.

headline: Matisse, the carver of light, at the Grand Palais in Paris
description: The delightful exhibition showcases later work by the artist who never stopped experimenting and features his famous cut-outs as well as monumental canvases.

headline: Escaping Putin's Russia: A father and daughter persecuted over a drawing fled to France
description: Alexei Moskalyov and his daughter, Maria, are symbolic victims of the Kremlin's repression. The father was jailed after posting an anti-war drawing his teenage daughter made at school. They have just arrived in France and are under humanitarian protection.

headline: War returns to southern Lebanon: 'Southern people have endured so many sacrifices'
Pope denounces widening gap between the rich and poor during visit to Monaco
Senegal parades Africa Cup of Nations trophy at the Stade de France to celebrate the 'only champions of Africa'
French police foil bomb attack in Paris outside Bank of America building
Iraq must not be drawn into 'escalation' of Middle East war, Macron says
Protesters rally in London to counter rise of British far right
Will the war in Iran advance or hinder Europe's energy transition?
Substack, a digital refuge for writers, accelerates its growth in France
Volodymyr Zelensky's exclusive interview with Le Monde on the war in Ukraine
At G7 summit, Rubio tries to deny the US is bogged down in Iran
Israel and Gulf states fear a hasty US exit from Iran
Singer Gims charged with 'aggravated money laundering'
In Nabatieh, southern Lebanon, 'no one ventures outside anymore apart from rescue teams'
description: Like 42 other rescue workers since the war began on March 2, 16-year-old Joude Souleiman was killed by an Israeli drone strike in Nabatieh, the second-largest Shiite city in southern Lebanon, where only a few hundred people out of 75,000 residents remain.

headline: The serial-poisoning mother and daughter inspired by 'Breaking Bad'
description: Castor beans, wolfsbane, antifreeze, rat poison: Carole D. and Amélie D. considered every possible method to poison the former's partner, Enrique B. The two women are set to stand trial before a criminal court in western France starting March 25.

headline: 'Trump would benefit from rereading Obama's 2015 speech on Iran'
description: The US president belatedly realized that the military option carried significant risks. He did not rely on his administration's recognized experts on Iran, notes Le Monde columnist Gilles Paris.

headline: Ukrainians' suffering must not be forgotten amid the war against Iran
The French people's vote is still up for grabs
Pierre Buhler, former diplomat: We are witnessing 'a worrying crack in the European edifice'
